The shortcut is functionally dead (no keydown handler in any .tsx file wires Alt+1-4 to any inspector navigation) — listing it in ShortcutsHelp would be documenting a non-existent shortcut.

audit_characteristic_flag_alignment.description: Cross-check of 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (13 members) vs 16b AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table (11 entries) and 16c fixture usage.

findings.detail: 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (line 1190) has 'provenance'. 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table (line 563) has flag: 'emits_provenance'. The backend will emit 'provenance' strings (via author-declared frozenset); the frontend's lookupAuditCharacteristic('provenance') will return null (unknown flag) and fall back to displaying the raw flag string. This is a pre-existing cross-plan mismatch that 16c inherits. The 16a test fixture at plan line 945 declares audit_characteristics = frozenset({'provenance', 'quarantine'}) — so real plugins can emit 'provenance', which the frontend has no metadata entry for.
findings.note: 16a's round-2 hardening note (line 2323) references a corrected 'emits_provenance' -> 'provenance' fix, but the backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS still contains 'provenance' (not 'emits_provenance'). The 16b test at line 407 checks lookupAuditCharacteristic('emits_provenance') — a flag the backend will never emit. These plans are not aligned on the flag name.

findings.detail: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS includes 'retention' (line 1191) and 'network' (line 1195) as author-declarable flags. 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table has no entries for 'retention' or 'network'. If a plugin author declares audit_characteristics = frozenset({'retention'}) or frozenset({'network'}), the backend passes validation (both are in VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS) and the API will return those strings. The frontend's lookupAuditCharacteristic('retention') and lookupAuditCharacteristic('network') both return null, resulting in the raw flag string being shown in chips/cards with no metadata. This is not a 16c-introduced problem — it is a pre-existing gap between 16a and 16b that 16c will silently expose.
findings.note: 16a plan narrative (line 221) lists 'network' as a manually declarable trait. 16b removed 'network' as a distinct flag because EXTERNAL_CALL.value maps to 'external_call'. But 'network' remains in VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS as a distinct declarable string separate from 'external_call'. An author can declare both.

findings.issue: 16c fixture values are internally consistent with 16a wire format
findings.detail: 16c's CatalogDrawer test fixtures use audit_characteristics: ['io_read', 'quarantine'] for csv and ['io_read', 'external_call'] for azure. Both 'io_read', 'quarantine', and 'external_call' exist in both 16a VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and 16b AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table. The 'network' chip filter test clicks { name: /network/i } which will match the 'Network call' label of the 'external_call' entry in lookupAuditCharacteristic. This is consistent.

finding.detail: The plan's ShortcutsHelp rewrite drops 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G — Open graph view' and 'Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y — Export YAML' from the shortcuts list. These shortcuts are currently wired: CommandPalette.tsx:143 ('Open graph view') and CommandPalette.tsx:154 ('Export YAML') dispatch them. The existing ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx tests at lines 18-30 assert both entries exist and will fail against the rewritten component. The plan says to 'Replace' the test file content — the incoming test suite does not include assertions for graph-view or YAML-export shortcuts, which means the replacement test file DELETES coverage for live, wired shortcuts. This is either intentional scope-creep removal of those shortcuts (not stated in plan scope) or an omission.

search_placeholder_test_match.detail: Partial regex match with case-insensitive flag will match the current placeholder text.

blocking_issues.title: 16b prerequisites not shipped — DataTrustTier, extended PluginSummary, auditCharacteristics.ts all absent from codebase
blocking_issues.detail: DataTrustTier is not in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/types/index.ts. PluginSummary lacks capability_tags, audit_characteristics, data_trust_tier, usage_when_to_use, usage_when_not_to_use. auditCharacteristics.ts does not exist. 16c's FilterChipStrip.tsx imports DataTrustTier from '@/types/index' and lookupAuditCharacteristic from './auditCharacteristics' — both will throw ImportError / tsc error. Tasks 1, 2, and 3 ALL fail to compile until 16b is merged.
blocking_issues.required_action: Merge 16b to the target branch before beginning 16c. Verify all 16b files exist: auditCharacteristics.ts, AuditCharacteristicIcon.tsx, TrustTierBadge.tsx, updated types/index.ts with DataTrustTier and extended PluginSummary.

blocking_issues.title: Alt+1-4 / 'Switch inspector tab' shortcut reintroduced — contradicts existing passing regression guard
blocking_issues.detail: ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx:36-37 (currently passing) asserts queryByText(/Switch inspector tab/i) is null. The plan's Task 4 ACTION_SHORTCUTS array includes { keys: 'Alt+1-4', action: 'Switch inspector tab (Spec/Graph/YAML/Runs)' }. Even though the plan replaces the test file, the new test file omits a negation for this entry. More critically: Alt+1-4 is not wired anywhere in the frontend — there is no keydown handler for this shortcut. Documenting an unwired shortcut in the help dialog is a user-facing lie.
blocking_issues.required_action: Remove the Alt+1-4 entry from ACTION_SHORTCUTS. If the plan intends to wire this shortcut, that belongs in a separate task with a wiring implementation — it is out of scope for 16c.

blocking_issues.title: Task 4 drops live wired shortcuts from ShortcutsHelp without wiring removal
blocking_issues.detail: The plan's ShortcutsHelp rewrite removes Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G ('Open graph view') and Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y ('Export YAML') from the flat list and does not place them in either ACTION_SHORTCUTS or REFERENCE_SHORTCUTS. These shortcuts are wired in CommandPalette.tsx (lines 143, 154) and have passing tests in ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx (lines 18-30). Dropping them from ShortcutsHelp without removing the keyboard wiring creates an undocumented-but-live shortcut situation.
blocking_issues.required_action: Either (a) add Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+G and Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Y to REFERENCE_SHORTCUTS or ACTION_SHORTCUTS in the plan's rewrite, or (b) explicitly remove those keyboard bindings from CommandPalette.tsx as part of this task and update the plan scope accordingly.

blocking_issues.title: 16a 'provenance' vs 16b 'emits_provenance' flag name mismatch causes silent frontend display degradation at runtime
blocking_issues.detail: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS contains 'provenance'. 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table uses flag: 'emits_provenance'. When a plugin declares audit_characteristics = frozenset({'provenance'}) (as in 16a's own test fixture at line 945), the API returns 'provenance'. The frontend's lookupAuditCharacteristic('provenance') returns null — the chip and any card display will show the raw string 'provenance' with no label, glyph, or tone. This misalignment is pre-existing between 16a and 16b but 16c will be the first surface to exercise it visually. The 16a round-2 note (line 2323) references a fixture fix but the backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and author-declaration mechanism still use 'provenance', not 'emits_provenance'.
blocking_issues.required_action: Align the flag name before shipping 16c. Either: (a) rename 'emits_provenance' to 'provenance' in 16b's auditCharacteristics.ts and all tests that reference 'emits_provenance'; or (b) rename 'provenance' to 'emits_provenance' in 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and all backend plugin declarations. Verify the 16a and 16b plans agree on a single canonical string.

warnings.title: 'retention' and 'network' in VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS have no frontend metadata entries
warnings.detail: 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS includes 'retention' (line 1191) and 'network' (line 1195) as author-declarable traits. 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table has no entries for either. A plugin author declaring these traits will produce API output that renders as raw unknown strings in the filter chip strip and PluginCard. This is an incomplete mapping that should be resolved in 16b before 16c lands.
warnings.required_action: Add 'retention' and 'network' entries to 16b's AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array, or remove them from 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS. The latter is preferable if 'network' was intentionally superseded by 'external_call'.

warnings.title: Task 4 test file replacement discards passing ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx assertions
warnings.detail: Current ShortcutsHelp.test.tsx has 3 passing tests: 'lists the plugin catalog shortcut', 'lists graph and YAML modal shortcuts', 'does not list retired inspector tab shortcuts'. The plan replaces the file content entirely with 4 new tests that omit the 'lists graph and YAML modal shortcuts' test (which tests live wired shortcuts). If those shortcuts are retained in the rewrite (per W1 / B3 fix), the test coverage must be preserved.
warnings.required_action: After resolving B3, ensure the replacement test file includes assertions for any shortcuts that remain in the component.

warnings.title: CatalogDrawer.test.tsx mock override in Phase 7B reshape describe block uses beforeEach with vi.mocked() but the module-level mock uses inline mockResolvedValue
warnings.detail: The existing CatalogDrawer.test.tsx (lines 7-25) wires mock values at the vi.mock() factory level (no vi.fn() — direct mockResolvedValue). The plan's new test suite uses vi.mocked(api.listSources).mockResolvedValue(...) in a beforeEach — this requires vi.clearAllMocks() between tests and the existing beforeEach does call vi.clearAllMocks(). However, the existing describe block's module-level mock factory returns mockResolvedValue without vi.fn() wrapping, meaning vi.mocked() may not work on those specific references. The plan adds a separate describe block so isolation is maintained, but if both describe blocks run together, the module-level factory mock will override the per-test mock in the new block. The plan should use a self-contained vi.mock factory within the new describe or ensure the import is from the same mocked module.
warnings.required_action: Verify the combined test file (both existing + new describe blocks) passes when run together. If the new describe block's beforeEach vi.mocked() overrides clash with the module-level factory, restructure using vi.resetModules() or a dedicated mock file.

nits.title: scorePlugin extended implementation does not include example_use in the target string
nits.detail: 16a ships example_use: str | None as one of the 6 new fields. The plan's extended scorePlugin at Task 3 Step 3 fuzzy-matches: plugin.name, plugin.description, plugin.usage_when_to_use, plugin.usage_when_not_to_use, plugin.capability_tags.join(' '). It does not include plugin.example_use. This may be intentional (example_use is a code snippet, not prose a user searches for) but is not explained.
nits.required_action: Document the omission with a comment in the scorePlugin implementation, or add example_use to the target string if users are expected to search by example content.

summary.verdict_rationale: 16c is internally coherent and well-specified. The plan correctly describes the intended diff against CatalogDrawer.tsx, ShortcutsHelp.tsx, and the two new components. There are no hallucinated symbols or fabricated APIs. However, four blocking issues prevent execution: (B1) 16b has not shipped — three type/file dependencies are absent from the codebase and will cause tsc failures in Tasks 1 and 3; (B2) Alt+1-4 is reintroduced as a documented shortcut but is not wired anywhere, contradicting an existing passing regression guard; (B3) two live wired shortcuts (graph view, YAML export) are silently dropped from ShortcutsHelp without removing their bindings; (B4) the 'provenance' vs 'emits_provenance' flag name mismatch between 16a and 16b will cause silent runtime degradation for any plugin that declares the 'provenance' audit characteristic. All four must be resolved before execution.
